channel.title: Manufacturing on DocB — Timeline
channel.description: Recent content in Manufacturing on DocB — Timeline

item.title: America Has 100 Rare Earth Operators. It Needs Thousands.
item.description: China's April 2025 export licenses turned a market constraint into a permission gate — and the US labor pool to bypass it doesn't exist.

item.title: Hyperscalers Reserved $450B in Chips Nobody Can Plug In
item.description: The constraint moved while everyone was buying chips — $450B in AI capex bottlenecks at five-year transformer queues, not silicon.

item.title: How a $7.9 Billion Order Made Your Next Laptop Worse
item.description: SK Hynix's record ASML deal is diverting DDR5 factories to AI memory — and your next machine ships with half the RAM at the same price

item.title: Your 3.5% Amazon Surcharge Began in a Texas Control Room
item.description: A Valero hydrotreater explosion at 98% refinery utilization eliminated America's last diesel buffer — and the cost landed on every Amazon seller's dashboard.

item.title: Every Machine in China's Battery Line Is Chinese
item.description: Gotion's 620-mile solid-state battery hits 90% yield while Northvolt and three European factories go bankrupt in a single quarter

item.title: The Slurry Problem
item.description: China's antimony and tungsten export controls are reaching Intel's fab floor through a liquid nobody talks about

item.title: We're Bolting Dummy Weights Into Our Most Advanced Fighters
item.description: F-35 Lot 17 delivered without radars. 11,000 munitions burned in 16 days. The US defence industrial base is failing at quality and surge simultaneously.
